meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on energy quantization and the photon concept
meta.core_formula: E = hf, K_max = hf - φ, E_n = -13.6/n² eV
meta.description: Photoelectric effect, Bohr model, atomic spectra, and wave-particle duality

patterns.name: Photoelectric Effect Analysis
patterns.recognition_signal: Light hitting metal, electron emission, work function mentioned
patterns.logic: Photon energy must exceed work function; excess becomes kinetic energy

efficiency_trick.name: Stopping Potential Conversion
steps: K_max in eV equals V₀ in volts numerically

pitfalls.name: Intensity misconception
pitfalls.description: Intensity affects number of electrons, not their energy

pitfalls.name: Unit confusion
pitfalls.description: Use consistent units: h = 4.136×10⁻¹⁵ eV·s for eV calculations

worked_example.title: Maximum Kinetic Energy
worked_example.problem_latex: Light with λ = 300 nm on metal with φ = 2.0 eV. Find K_max.

professor_tips: The photoelectric effect demonstrates the particle nature of light
Intensity affects number of photoelectrons, frequency affects their energy
Lyman series: UV, Balmer: visible, Paschen: IR

confidence_checklist: I can apply the photoelectric equation
I understand the difference between classical and quantum predictions
I can calculate hydrogen energy levels
I can relate photon energy to wavelength
I understand atomic spectra and series
